title: Riggers
description: Set up or repair rigging for construction projects, manufacturing plants, logging yards, ships and shipyards, or for the entertainment industry.

tasks.text: Test rigging to ensure safety and reliability.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Signal or verbally direct workers engaged in hoisting and moving loads to ensure safety of workers and materials.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Control movement of heavy equipment through narrow openings or confined spaces, using chainfalls, gin poles, gallows frames, and other equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Tilt, dip, and turn suspended loads to maneuver over, under, or around obstacles, using multi-point suspension techniques.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select gear, such as cables, pulleys, and winches, according to load weights and sizes, facilities, and work schedules.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dismantle and store rigging equipment after use.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach loads to rigging to provide support or prepare them for moving, using hand and power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manipulate rigging lines, hoists, and pulling gear to move or support materials, such as heavy equipment, ships, or theatrical sets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load machines onto trucks to prepare for transportation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach pulleys and blocks to fixed overhead structures, such as beams, ceilings, and gin pole booms, using bolts and clamps.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fabricate, set up, and repair rigging, supporting structures, hoists, and pulling gear, using hand and power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and dress machine surfaces and component parts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install ground rigging for yarding lines, attaching chokers to logs and to the lines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
